title: OREGON VITAL RECORDS - 211info
text: OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION OREGON VITAL RECORDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Maintains and issues all vital records for the State of Oregon, including birth, death, marriage, domestic partnerships and divorce records. The office has birth and death records dating from July 1903, marriage records from 1906, and divorce certificates (not decrees) from 1925. For birth and death records within the first 6 months of issuance, copies are also available at the local County Health Department. Domestic Partnership Declaration forms can be obtained by mail through the Center for Health Statistics or from individual County Clerk's offices. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Birth, marriage, divorce, and domestic partnership records provided to immediate family and legal representatives. Death records provided to family members and persons with a personal or property right. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (888) 896-4988 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (971) 673-1222 Main phone (971) 673-1299 FAX 711 TTY/TTD (800) 422-6012 Toll-free Orders Hotline (888) 896-4988 General Information (971) 673-1190 Fax (971) 673-1203 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call or visit website for information. Call hotline for order information. Vital records may be ordered online, by mail, by phone or by walking in to any Vital Records office. FEES: -Online orders: $43.25 certified copy, $25 for each additional copy -Phone orders: $44.95 for one record, $25 for each additional copy -Mail orders: $25 for one record (allow 8-10 weeks for processing), $25 for each additional copy -Rush mail orders (send order form by overnight delivery and pay with a money order): $25 for one record, $25 for each additional copy -Walk-in orders: $28 for one record, $25 for each additional copy DOCUMENTS: Social security number and valid ID. Call about other documentation that may be necessary. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Birth Certificates Census Data Death Related Records/Permits Divorce Records Marriage Certificates Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ALERT IMMUNIZATION INFORMATION SYSTEM COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - OREGON SCREENWISE PROGRAM CONSUMER REGULATION - BIRTH CERTIFICATES CONSUMER REGULATION - SENIOR FARM DIRECT APPLICATION FOR FARMERS & FARM STANDS DISASTER SERVICES - STATE EMERGENCY REGISTRY OF VOLUNTEERS IN OREGON DISASTER SERVICES - WINTER WEATHER ADVISORIES ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AND IMPROVEMENT - CLIMATE CHANGE AND HEALTH FOOD - SENIOR FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM FOOD - WIC FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - BREAST AND CERVICAL CANCER TREATMENT HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON IMMUNIZATION PROGRAM HUMAN REPRODUCTION - A SAFE PLACE FOR NEWBORNS HUMAN REPRODUCTION - REPRODUCTIVE AND SEXUAL HEALTH HUMAN REPRODUCTION - STATE OF OREGON CONDOM DELIVERY PROGRAM INFORMATION SERVICES - DEATH WITH DIGNITY ACT MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - PSILOCYBIN SERVICES PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON PRESCRIPTION DRUG PROGRAM ARRAYRX PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON WIC ADMINISTRATION PUBLIC HEALTH - COMMUNICABLE DISEASE INFORMATION PUBLIC HEALTH - ENVIRONMENTAL PUBLIC HEALTH PUBLIC HEALTH - LOCAL PUBLIC HEALTH AUTHORITY DIRECTORY PUBLIC SAFETY - OHA DRINKING WATER DATA ONLINE REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - EARLY HEARING DETECTION AND INTERVENTION SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - OREGON MEDICAL MARIJUANA PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES FOR CHILDREN SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - OREGON HOPELINE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - TOBACCO QUIT LINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
